'{"abstract": "VCF files of loci in approximate linkage equilibrium for two common spruce budworm larval parasitoids: Apanteles fumiferanae (Hymenoptera: Braconidae) and Glypta fumiferanae (Hymenoptera: Ichneumonidae)

Spruce budworm larvae were collected from late May to mid-July 2014 from 29 sites located within and around six distinct outbreak areas in the province of Qu\\u00e9bec, Canada.\\u00a0Larvae were reared individually on artificial diet until\\u00a0egression of Apanteles fumiferanae and Glypta fumiferanae\\u00a0larvae. Adult parasitoids\\u00a0were then stored individually in dry conditions at -80\\u00b0C prior to DNA extraction.\\u00a0Because both\\u00a0A. fumiferanae\\u00a0and\\u00a0G. fumiferanae\\u00a0are haplodiploid, total genomic DNA was extracted only from females (1\\u201323 females per species per site).\\u00a0Genotyping-by-sequencing libraries were constructed using the protocol of\\u00a0Poland, Brown, Sorrells, and Jannink (2012), and then\\u00a0single-end\\u00a0sequenced\\u00a0(100 bp reads)\\u00a0using an Illumina HiSeq2000.\\u00a0Because there is no reference genome available for\\u00a0A. fumiferanae\\u00a0and\\u00a0G. fumiferanae, we used the TASSEL 3.0 UNEAK\\u00a0(Universal Network-Enabled Analysis Kit)\\u00a0pipeline for single-nucleotide-polymorphism (SNP) calling\\u00a0(Lu et al., 2013). Only\\u00a0sequences with five or more reads\\u00a0over all\\u00a0individuals were kept, creating a master list of sequences\\u00a0which serves as a\\u00a0\'pseudo reference genome\'. During alignment, a 0.03 error tolerance rate.\\u00a0SNP loci with a minor allele frequency < 5% were removed.\\u00a0Genotypes were corrected using the program\\u00a0Uneak_filter\\u00a0to\\u00a0consider\\u00a0low allele frequencies that could have resulted from sequencing errors. Loci absent in > 25% of individuals and individuals with > 10% missing loci were pruned out of the data matrix using TASSEL 5. We then created a pruned dataset of SNP loci for each species by removing SNPs in high linkage disequilibrium (correlation coefficient\\u00a0\\u2265 0.3) were dropped to remove highly correlated variants.

Periodic and spatially synchronous outbreaks of insect pests have dramatic consequences for boreal and sub-boreal forests. Within these multitrophic systems, parasitoids\\u00a0can\\u00a0be stabilizing agents by dispersing toward patches containing higher\\u00a0host\\u00a0density (the so-called\\u00a0birdfeeder effect). However, we know little about the dispersal abilities of parasitoids in continuous forested landscapes, limiting our understanding of the spatiotemporal dynamics of host\\u2013parasitoid systems, and constraining our ability to predict forest resilience in the context of global changes. In this study, we investigate the spatial genetic structure and spatial variation in genetic diversity of two important species of spruce budworm larval parasitoids during outbreaks:\\u00a0Apanteles fumiferanae\\u00a0Viereck (Braconidae)\\u00a0and\\u00a0Glypta fumiferanae\\u00a0(Viereck) (Ichneumonidae). Using parasitoids sampled in 2014\\u00a0from\\u00a026 and 29\\u00a0locations across a study area of 350,000 km2, we identified 1,012 and 992 neutral SNP loci for\\u00a0A. fumiferanae\\u00a0(N\\u00a0=\\u00a0279 individuals) and\\u00a0G.\\u00a0fumiferanae\\u00a0(N\\u00a0=\\u00a0382), respectively. Using DAPC,\\u00a0PCA,\\u00a0AMOVA, and IBD\\u00a0analyses, we found evidence for panmixia and high genetic connectivity for both species, matching the previously described genetic structure of the spruce budworm within the same context, suggesting similar effective dispersal during outbreaks\\u00a0and high parasitoid population densities between outbreaks. We also found a significant negative relationship between genetic diversity and latitude for\\u00a0A.\\u00a0fumiferanae\\u00a0but not for\\u00a0G. fumiferanae, suggesting that northern range limits may vary by species within the spruce budworm parasitoid community. These spatial dynamics should be considered when predicting future insect outbreak severities in boreal landscapes.

We provide two VCF files of\\u00a0loci in approximate linkage equilibrium: one for\\u00a0Apanteles fumiferanae\\u00a0(Apanteles_fumiferanae_279inds_1056loci_LD_filtered.vcf) and one for\\u00a0Glypta fumiferanae (Glypta_fumiferanae_382inds_1044loci_LD_filtered.vcf).\\u00a0We also provide the associated metadata from Table 1 of the article\\u00a0(outbreak areas, sites numbers, and latitude and longitude) in two text files (Apanteles_fumiferanae_Metadata.txt and\\u00a0Glypta_fumiferanae_Metadata.txt).

_x000D_\\n

Funding provided by: Fonds Qu\\u00e9b\\u00e9cois de la Recherche sur la Nature et les Technologies
Crossref Funder Registry ID: http://dx.doi.org/10.13039/501100003150
Award Number: Team grant: 174142
